claim: Storage upload returns undefined 'data' object, causing TypeError when accessing data.path
evidence: Line 175: return { ...rest, storage_path: data?.path }; — 'data' is undefined because storage.upload() returns { data, error } but only error is checked. When upload succeeds, data is not destructured.
runtimeTrigger: POST /api/submit-surat with attachments containing data_url → storage upload succeeds but data.path access fails → TypeError → 500 error → submission fails

claim: NIK enumeration via timing attack — rate limiter only checks AFTER NIK format validation
evidence: Lines 106-117: isNikSearch check happens before rateLimitNik() call. Attacker can probe NIK format validity (16 digits) without hitting rate limit by sending invalid formats first.
runtimeTrigger: POST /api/verify-surat with { q: '1234567890123456' } (valid format) vs { q: '123' } (invalid) → different response times reveal NIK format validity before rate limit kicks in

claim: Warga ownership check uses loose string comparison — NIK mismatch due to type coercion
evidence: Line 163: const recordNik = String(suratRow.nik ?? ''); Line 164: if (auth.wargaNik !== recordNik) — if suratRow.nik is numeric and auth.wargaNik is string, String() coercion may not match due to leading zeros in NIK
runtimeTrigger: Warga with NIK '0312345678901234' (leading zero) tries to download surat where DB stored nik as bigint 312345678901234 → String(312345678901234) = '312345678901234' !== '0312345678901234' → false negative (legitimate access denied) OR false positive if attacker guesses numeric form

claim: Rate limit increment failure is silently swallowed — allows unlimited submissions after DB error
evidence: Lines 214-218: incrementRateLimit() catches all errors and only logs warning. If Supabase RPC fails, rate limit counter is not incremented → user can submit unlimited requests.
runtimeTrigger: POST /api/submit-surat → Supabase RPC 'increment_submission_count' fails (network/DB error) → catch block swallows error → rate limit not enforced → user can submit 10+ requests in 24h window

claim: Phone number normalization logic has edge case — numbers starting with '62' but not '628' are passed through unchanged
evidence: Line 128: const normalized = raw.startsWith('0') ? '62' + raw.slice(1) : raw.startsWith('62') ? raw : raw; — if raw = '6212345' (starts with 62 but not 628), it's returned as-is without validation. Fonnte expects 628xxx format.
runtimeTrigger: POST /api/send-wa with target='6212345' → normalized='6212345' → Fonnte API rejects invalid format → WA not sent but endpoint returns success

claim: BigInt conversion loses precision for amounts > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER
evidence: Line 123: amount: BigInt(Math.round(Number(amount))) — if amount is already a large number (e.g., from JSON), Number() conversion may lose precision before BigInt() is applied. Should parse directly to BigInt.
runtimeTrigger: POST /api/keuangan/entries with amount=9007199254740993 (> MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) → Number(amount) rounds to 9007199254740992 → BigInt() stores wrong value → financial data corruption

claim: Missing role check on GET /api/wilayah — verifyAdmin allows all admin roles to read wilayah data
evidence: Line 20: router.get('/', verifyAdmin, (req, res) — verifyAdmin only checks session validity, not role. All authenticated admins (including Verifikator) can read wilayah data. Other endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE) also lack role checks.
runtimeTrigger: Verifikator role (lowest privilege) can GET /api/wilayah → reads all administrative area data including sensitive location info

claim: Audit log insert failure is silently ignored — no record of admin actions if DB fails
evidence: Lines 227-232: await sb.from('audit_log').insert(...) — no error handling. If insert fails, admin action (status change) succeeds but audit trail is lost → compliance violation (UU PDP).
runtimeTrigger: PUT /api/pengaduan/admin/:ticket → pengaduan updated successfully → audit_log insert fails (DB constraint/network) → no audit record → admin action untracked

claim: Production server starts with wildcard CORS when ALLOWED_ORIGIN is not set — allows any origin to make authenticated requests
evidence: Lines 48-54: if (NODE_ENV === 'production' && (!ALLOWED_ORIGIN || ALLOWED_ORIGIN === '*')) { process.exit( check exists but only prevents startup. If ALLOWED_ORIGIN is set to empty string (not null/undefined), check passes but line 126 uses 'origin: ALLOWED_ORIGIN || undefined' → undefined = wildcard CORS.
runtimeTrigger: Start server with ALLOWED_ORIGIN='' (empty string) in production → startup check passes (empty string is truthy) → cors({ origin: '' || undefined }) → origin: undefined → wildcard CORS → any origin can send credentials

claim: Public GET /api/inventaris endpoint exposes internal asset data without authentication
evidence: Line 26: router.get('/', async (req, res) — no auth middleware. Returns asset details including acquisition_value, current_value, responsible, location. Sensitive financial and operational data exposed to unauthenticated users.
runtimeTrigger: GET /api/inventaris?limit=1000 → returns all active assets with financial values, locations, responsible parties → data harvesting by unauthenticated attacker
